title: Executive Order 12662—Implementing the United States-Canada Free-Trade Implementation Act

flags.justification: Narrow, distributed implementation provisions (notice requirements, Secretariat establishment, panel-decision acceptance).
Sec. 1's 75-day notice requirement for standards-related rules is a specific, transparency-enhancing procedural requirement, longer than standard APA notice periods.
Multiple related implementation provisions all coherently implement one trade agreement.

The 'urgent circumstances' exception to the notice requirement is bounded by the specifically-defined term 'legitimate domestic objective' (Sec. 1(b)(1): health, safety, security, environment, or consumer interests).
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
Implements a permanent trade agreement, evergreen by nature; the U.S.-Canada FTA became the direct template for NAFTA.

Sec. 3 commits the President to 'accept, as a whole, all decisions of binational panels and extraordinary challenge committees' — a substantial advance commitment to accept adjudication by an internationally-constituted body in place of ordinary domestic judicial review of certain trade-remedy determinations, though implementing a congressionally-approved international agreement.

Sec. 4 states the order 'does not create any right or benefit... enforceable at law by a party against the United States' — the same no-private-right-of-action pattern found in EO 12114 and EO 12615, here compounding the binational-panel commitment's further limitation on domestic judicial recourse.

Implements the historic U.S.-Canada Free-Trade Agreement, the direct template for NAFTA; the binational panel dispute-resolution mechanism itself was a genuinely novel departure from purely domestic trade-remedy review.
A clean, single-purpose Commerce Department Secretariat designation.
Sec. 1(c)'s exclusion of agricultural and food goods from the extended-notice requirement is narrow and specific, consistent with the underlying agreement's own chapter structure.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 3/22 = 13.6%. Implements the direct predecessor to NAFTA. Third confirmed instance of the no-private-right-of-action pattern (see EO 12114, EO 12615), here paired with a distinctive additional feature: an advance commitment to accept binational panel adjudication in place of domestic judicial review.
